channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Israel and Iran trade strikes, threatening to drag region back to full-scale war
item.description: Israel and Iran traded fire early Monday in retaliatory strikes that threatened to drag the wider Middle East back into a full-scale war, while Yemen's Houthi rebels warned they would target Israel-affiliated ships in the Red Sea.
item.content:encoded:
Israel and Iran traded fire early Monday in retaliatory strikes that threatened to drag the wider Middle East back into a full-scale war, while Yemen's Houthi rebels warned they would target Israel-affiliated ships in the Red Sea.

(Image credit: Rami Shlush)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A 7.8 magnitude earthquake rocks the southern Philippines
item.description: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake centered at sea shook part of the southern Philippines early Monday, causing damage, knocking down power and setting off 1-meter (3-foot) tsunami waves, officials said.
item.content:encoded:
A magnitude 7.8 earthquake centered at sea shook part of the southern Philippines early Monday, causing damage, knocking down power and setting off 1-meter (3-foot) tsunami waves, officials said.

(Image credit: Ernesto Torres Jr/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump rejects idea that Iran betrays his 'no new wars' campaign message
item.description: President Trump is dismissing the idea that launching the war with Iran betrayed his refrain of "No new wars" as he campaigned for the White House in 2024.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump is dismissing the idea that launching the war with Iran betrayed his refrain of "No new wars" as he campaigned for the White House in 2024.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Can birds outsing traffic? Some are trying
item.description: New research from scientists at the Centre for Ecological Research in Hungary finds that some birds living in cities are changing their songs to compete with traffic and other urban noise.
item.content:encoded: New research from scientists at the Centre for Ecological Research in Hungary finds that some birds living in cities are changing their songs to compete with traffic and other urban noise.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Israel hits Beirut's suburbs in retaliatory attack against Hezbollah
item.description: The attack comes after Hezbollah struck Israel's military with fighter drones, according to Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's office.
item.content:encoded:
The attack comes after Hezbollah struck Israel's military with fighter drones, according to Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's office.

(Image credit: AFP)
item.dc:creator: Jordan-Marie Smith

item.title: Israel says Iran launched a missile at it, in a first during fragile ceasefire
item.description: The attack would be the first since a fragile ceasefire took effect in early April, complicating mediation efforts for a deal to end the war.
item.content:encoded:
The attack would be the first since a fragile ceasefire took effect in early April, complicating mediation efforts for a deal to end the war.

(Image credit: Hassan Ammar)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Alexander Zverev wins the French Open to finally earn a 1st Grand Slam title
item.description: After Italian Flavio Cobolli missed an overhead on the second championship point of the five-set encounter, Zverev dropped on his back and began sobbing.
item.content:encoded:
After Italian Flavio Cobolli missed an overhead on the second championship point of the five-set encounter, Zverev dropped on his back and began sobbing.

(Image credit: Aurelien Morissard)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.K. deputy prime minister: JD Vance was wrong to blame teen's murder on immigration
item.description: Britain's deputy prime minister says he told U.S. Vice President JD Vance he was wrong to blame immigration for the death of a university student who was handcuffed as he lay dying from a stab wound.
item.content:encoded:
Britain's deputy prime minister says he told U.S. Vice President JD Vance he was wrong to blame immigration for the death of a university student who was handcuffed as he lay dying from a stab wound.

(Image credit: Suzanne Plunkett)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Armenians vote in general election watched closely by Russia and the West
item.description: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan and his governing party are looking for a strong mandate for a new geopolitical course for Armenia. The opposition includes some parties that are vocally pro-Russia.
item.content:encoded:
Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan and his governing party are looking for a strong mandate for a new geopolitical course for Armenia. The opposition includes some parties that are vocally pro-Russia.

(Image credit: Anthony Pizzoferrato)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: It's one of the world's most isolated islands. Here come the bulldozers
item.description: The Indian government is spending $9 billion to create a megaport, airport and city on this remote island. Critics fear the impact on pristine forests and the lives of indigenous inhabitants.
item.content:encoded:
The Indian government is spending $9 billion to create a megaport, airport and city on this remote island. Critics fear the impact on pristine forests and the lives of indigenous inhabitants.

(Image credit: Omkar Khandekar/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Omkar Khandekar
